_validation_note: Validation completed 2026-05-23. Window: 2026-05-16 to 2026-05-23. 7 stories assessed; 4 verified, 3 dropped. Key strips: $1M civil penalty claim (HB 239, not on any source); $46B LNG figure (unverified); Cook Inlet/data-center nexus (not in LNG adjournment sources — must use background context from ADN May 5 if needed). Stories 5, 6, 7 dropped.

verified_stories.story_title: SB 250 Data-Center Utility Bill Passes Senate, Dies in House at Adjournment
verified_stories.softening_note: Say 'did not advance past House committee before adjournment' not 'died.' ADN adjournment article does NOT mention SB 250 — cannot be used to corroborate SB 250 specifics.
confirmed_claims: SB 250 relates to utility service for data centers (sponsored by Sen. Löki Tobin)
Senate passed SB 250 Y14 N5 E1 on May 16, 2026
House Community and Regional Affairs committee heard and held SB 250 on May 18, 2026
Bill did not advance past House committee before session adjourned May 20

verified_stories.story_title: Legislature Passes HB 239 Criminalizing AI-Generated Child Sexual Abuse Material; Bill Awaits Dunleavy
verified_stories.softening_note: CORRECTION: Senate passed May 19 (not May 20). House passed 39-1 on May 20. STRIP: $1 million civil penalty per incident claim — NOT found on any fetched source. Governor's office flagged concerns is confirmed.
confirmed_claims: HB 239 passed the Alaska Senate 20-0 on May 19, 2026
HB 239 passed the Alaska House with concurrence 39-1 on May 20, 2026
Bill criminalizes AI-generated obscene child sexual abuse material
Bill raises Alaska age of sexual consent to 18
Governor's office expressed concerns about inadequate deliberation and potential legal problems
Bill awaiting governor's action (sign, veto, or allow to become law without signature)
stripped_claims: $1 million civil penalty per incident on AI platforms — NOT FOUND on any source, do not use

verified_stories.story_title: Alaska Legislature Adjourns Regular Session, Opens Special Session on LNG Pipeline
verified_stories.softening_note: STRIP: '$46 billion LNG pipeline' figure — not found on any fetched source. STRIP: 'Cook Inlet gas supply depleted for data centers' — not in LNG/adjournment sources. If connecting LNG to data center energy supply, writer must use background context from ADN May 5 article (Chugach/GVEA Cook Inlet gas statements) and label it as such. Core facts verified: Legislature adjourned May 20, special session opened May 21 on Alaska LNG pipeline tax breaks.
confirmed_claims: Alaska Legislature adjourned regular session on May 20, 2026
Governor Dunleavy called a special session beginning May 21, 2026 focused on the Alaska LNG project
Special session centers on replacing a roughly $1 billion annual property tax with a volume-based throughput tax
Three competing LNG tax proposals: governor's (6-cent volumetric), House substitute (15-cent plus Fairbanks spur line and $40M community impact fund), Senate substitute (40-cent)
stripped_claims: '$46 billion LNG pipeline' — dollar figure not found in any fetched source
'Cook Inlet gas supply depleted for data centers' — not in any fetched LNG/adjournment source (use ADN May 5 as background context if needed)

verified_stories.story_title: Alaska Autonomous Vehicle Bill (HB 217) Stalls in Senate, Dies at Adjournment
verified_stories.softening_note: CORRECTION: House vote was Y39 E1 (39 yes, 1 excused) — not '39-1' which implies a no vote. Senate State Affairs committee 2-Do Not Pass / 1-Do Pass on May 17 confirmed. Bill did not advance past Senate committee before adjournment May 20.
confirmed_claims: HB 217 addresses autonomous vehicle regulation and commercial driver licensing in Alaska
Senate State Affairs Committee issued 2-Do Not Pass / 1-Do Pass report on May 17, 2026
House passed HB 217 Y39 E1 (39 yes, 1 excused) on April 30, 2026
Bill did not advance past Senate committee before session adjourned May 20, 2026
stripped_claims: 'passed House 39-1' — correct to 'Y39 E1' (no member voted against; 1 excused)

sources.outlet: Alaska Public Media (background context, outside window)

validation_warnings: HB 239: Senate passage date was May 19, not May 20. Correct in post.
HB 217: House vote was Y39 E1 (no member voted against), not 39-1.
LNG story: $46B figure unverified; Cook Inlet/data center nexus must use ADN May 5 background context if used at all.
HB 239: $1M civil penalty claim is unverified and must be stripped entirely.
ADN adjournment article cannot corroborate SB 250 specifics — it doesn't mention the bill.
